questions.prompt: The Song dynasty's revival of the civil service examination system based on Neo-Confucian texts most directly contributed to which long-term outcome?
choices: The emergence of a hereditary warrior aristocracy
The creation of a meritocratic scholar-gentry class
The collapse of imperial bureaucratic authority
The rise of Buddhist monastic political power
questions.correctText: The creation of a meritocratic scholar-gentry class

questions.prompt: Which of the following best explains the role of Sufism in the expansion of Islam between 1200 and 1450?
choices: It enforced strict legal interpretations through state power
It facilitated conversion by emphasizing mystical and personal devotion that adapted to local cultures
It rejected all forms of trade and economic activity
It united Sunni and Shia communities under a single caliph
questions.correctText: It facilitated conversion by emphasizing mystical and personal devotion that adapted to local cultures

questions.prompt: The Delhi Sultanate's rule over a predominantly Hindu population is best characterized as
choices: A complete forced conversion of subject populations
A pragmatic accommodation that maintained Muslim political dominance while tolerating local religious practices
A federal system that divided power evenly between Hindus and Muslims
A theocracy that abolished caste distinctions
questions.correctText: A pragmatic accommodation that maintained Muslim political dominance while tolerating local religious practices

questions.prompt: The bhakti movement in South Asia and Sufism in the Islamic world are most comparable in that both
choices: Established centralized priestly hierarchies
Emphasized direct, emotional, and personal devotion accessible to ordinary believers
Required pilgrimage to a single holy city
Rejected the use of vernacular languages
questions.correctText: Emphasized direct, emotional, and personal devotion accessible to ordinary believers

questions.prompt: Angkor Wat's transformation from a Hindu to a Buddhist temple complex best illustrates which broader pattern in pre-1450 Southeast Asia?
choices: The military conquest of South Asia by Khmer rulers
The syncretic blending of South Asian religious traditions adapted to local political needs
The rejection of all foreign cultural influences
The dominance of Confucian state ideology
questions.correctText: The syncretic blending of South Asian religious traditions adapted to local political needs

questions.prompt: The Mexica (Aztec) tribute system most directly served to
choices: Eliminate ethnic distinctions among subject peoples
Extract resources and labor from conquered city-states to support the imperial capital
Establish a market economy based on free exchange
Promote intermarriage between elite and commoner classes
questions.correctText: Extract resources and labor from conquered city-states to support the imperial capital

questions.prompt: The Inca mit'a system is most similar to which other labor system in this period?
choices: The chattel slavery of the Atlantic World
The corvée labor obligations of medieval European peasants to their lords
The wage labor of industrial Britain
The indentured servitude of the Indian Ocean basin
questions.correctText: The corvée labor obligations of medieval European peasants to their lords

questions.prompt: Mansa Musa's 1324-25 hajj is most useful to historians as evidence of
choices: The military weakness of Mali against North African states
Mali's integration into trans-Saharan and Mediterranean commercial and diplomatic networks
The decline of Islam in West Africa
The Mali Empire's rejection of gold currency
questions.correctText: Mali's integration into trans-Saharan and Mediterranean commercial and diplomatic networks

questions.prompt: Great Zimbabwe's stone architecture and trade artifacts most directly demonstrate
choices: The isolation of southern Africa from world trade
Sub-Saharan African participation in long-distance Indian Ocean commerce
The dominance of European traders in interior Africa
The absence of state-level organization south of the Sahara
questions.correctText: Sub-Saharan African participation in long-distance Indian Ocean commerce

questions.prompt: European feudalism and Japanese feudalism in this period are best compared in that both
choices: Were ruled by centralized absolute monarchs
Featured decentralized political authority with reciprocal obligations between warrior elites and laborers
Abolished hereditary privilege
Were based on monastic landholding
questions.correctText: Featured decentralized political authority with reciprocal obligations between warrior elites and laborers

questions.prompt: The Mongol Yuan dynasty's policies in China are best characterized as
choices: A complete Sinicization that abolished Mongol identity
A racial hierarchy that placed Mongols above Han Chinese in government service
An egalitarian system that granted Han Chinese full political equality
A theocracy ruled by Tibetan Buddhist clergy
questions.correctText: A racial hierarchy that placed Mongols above Han Chinese in government service

questions.prompt: The term 'Pax Mongolica' most directly refers to
choices: A period of religious uniformity across Eurasia
A period of relative stability under Mongol rule that facilitated long-distance trade and cultural exchange across Eurasia
A peace treaty ending the Crusades
A military alliance between China and Russia
questions.correctText: A period of relative stability under Mongol rule that facilitated long-distance trade and cultural exchange across Eurasia

questions.prompt: The spread of the Black Death from Central Asia to Europe is best understood as a consequence of
choices: The collapse of the Silk Roads after 1300
Intensified Eurasian trade and movement under the Mongol Empire
European colonization of the Americas
The decline of Indian Ocean shipping
questions.correctText: Intensified Eurasian trade and movement under the Mongol Empire

questions.prompt: The dhow and the junk are most useful as evidence of
choices: European technological superiority over Asian shipbuilders
Maritime technology adapted to monsoon-driven Indian Ocean and South China Sea trade
The absence of trade in the pre-modern Indian Ocean
Military innovations of the Mongol Empire
questions.correctText: Maritime technology adapted to monsoon-driven Indian Ocean and South China Sea trade

questions.prompt: The Swahili Coast city-states are best characterized as
choices: Inland agricultural kingdoms isolated from coastal trade
Cosmopolitan Islamic trading entrepôts linking East Africa to the Indian Ocean world
Portuguese colonial settlements
Chinese tributary states
questions.correctText: Cosmopolitan Islamic trading entrepôts linking East Africa to the Indian Ocean world

questions.prompt: The introduction of the camel saddle most directly enabled
choices: The decline of trans-Saharan trade
The expansion of large-scale trans-Saharan caravan commerce
The collapse of the Mali Empire
The Christianization of North Africa
questions.correctText: The expansion of large-scale trans-Saharan caravan commerce

questions.prompt: The travels of Ibn Battuta in the fourteenth century are most useful to historians as evidence of
choices: The political unification of the Islamic world under a single caliph
The existence of a cosmopolitan Dar al-Islam linked by shared law, language, and institutions
The total isolation of West Africa from the Mediterranean
The Mongol conquest of Mecca and Medina
questions.correctText: The existence of a cosmopolitan Dar al-Islam linked by shared law, language, and institutions

questions.prompt: Which of the following best explains the demographic impact of the Black Death on fourteenth-century Europe?
choices: It reinforced the power of feudal lords over peasants
Sharp labor shortages weakened serfdom and raised real wages for surviving workers
It led to the immediate abolition of Christianity
It caused the European population to double within a generation
questions.correctText: Sharp labor shortages weakened serfdom and raised real wages for surviving workers

questions.prompt: Gunpowder weaponry's spread from Song China across Eurasia by 1450 is best categorized as an example of
choices: Independent invention in multiple isolated cultures
Technological diffusion enabled by Mongol and trade-route connections
European colonization of East Asia
The decline of long-distance exchange
questions.correctText: Technological diffusion enabled by Mongol and trade-route connections

questions.prompt: Mehmed II's 1453 conquest of Constantinople is best understood as marking
choices: The beginning of Byzantine military dominance
The end of the Byzantine Empire and consolidation of Ottoman power across the eastern Mediterranean
The fall of the Ottoman dynasty
The Christianization of Anatolia
questions.correctText: The end of the Byzantine Empire and consolidation of Ottoman power across the eastern Mediterranean

questions.prompt: The Ottoman devshirme system most directly served to
choices: Create a hereditary nobility independent of the sultan
Recruit Christian boys to staff the elite Janissary corps and imperial administration loyal to the sultan
Free Christian populations from Ottoman taxation
Establish a parliamentary system
questions.correctText: Recruit Christian boys to staff the elite Janissary corps and imperial administration loyal to the sultan

questions.prompt: The Ottoman millet system is best characterized as
choices: Forced conversion of all non-Muslims to Islam
A policy granting religious minorities communal self-government in exchange for loyalty and taxes
Complete legal equality between Muslims and non-Muslims
The expulsion of non-Muslims from the empire
questions.correctText: A policy granting religious minorities communal self-government in exchange for loyalty and taxes

questions.prompt: The Safavid Empire's establishment of Twelver Shia Islam as a state religion under Shah Ismail most directly contributed to
choices: The unification of Sunni and Shia Muslims
A long-term sectarian rivalry with the Sunni Ottoman Empire that shaped Southwest Asian politics
The conversion of Iran to Christianity
Safavid control of North Africa
questions.correctText: A long-term sectarian rivalry with the Sunni Ottoman Empire that shaped Southwest Asian politics

questions.prompt: Akbar's policies of religious tolerance, including the abolition of the jizya tax, are best understood as
choices: An attempt to convert the empire to Hinduism
A pragmatic strategy to legitimize Mughal rule over a religiously diverse population
A response to European missionary pressure
Evidence that the Mughals abandoned Islam
questions.correctText: A pragmatic strategy to legitimize Mughal rule over a religiously diverse population

questions.prompt: The reign of Aurangzeb (1658-1707) is often contrasted with that of Akbar because Aurangzeb
choices: Expanded religious tolerance and abolished new taxes on Hindus
Reinstated the jizya and pursued stricter Sunni Muslim orthodoxy, contributing to internal unrest
Converted the empire to Christianity
Voluntarily dissolved the Mughal dynasty
questions.correctText: Reinstated the jizya and pursued stricter Sunni Muslim orthodoxy, contributing to internal unrest

questions.prompt: The construction of the Taj Mahal under Shah Jahan most directly reflects
choices: Persian, Islamic, and Indian artistic synthesis characteristic of Mughal imperial patronage
Mughal rejection of all non-Indian artistic traditions
European Gothic architectural influence
The decline of imperial wealth
questions.correctText: Persian, Islamic, and Indian artistic synthesis characteristic of Mughal imperial patronage

questions.prompt: The Ming voyages of Zheng He (1405-1433) ended primarily because
choices: Chinese ships were defeated in battle by Portuguese fleets
Confucian court officials viewed the voyages as costly and contrary to traditional priorities of land-based defense and agriculture
Zheng He converted to Islam and refused to return
The voyages bankrupted the empire instantly
questions.correctText: Confucian court officials viewed the voyages as costly and contrary to traditional priorities of land-based defense and agriculture

questions.prompt: The transition from Ming to Qing in 1644 is best characterized as
choices: A peaceful constitutional reform
A foreign Manchu conquest that nonetheless preserved many Confucian bureaucratic structures
A democratic revolution
The Christianization of China
questions.correctText: A foreign Manchu conquest that nonetheless preserved many Confucian bureaucratic structures

questions.prompt: Ivan IV's policies and Peter the Great's reforms shared which common goal?
choices: Decentralizing power into the hands of local nobles
Strengthening tsarist autocratic authority and expanding Russian state power
Granting serfs full freedom and political rights
Adopting parliamentary democracy
questions.correctText: Strengthening tsarist autocratic authority and expanding Russian state power

questions.prompt: Peter the Great's westernization program is best characterized as
choices: A rejection of all Western influences
Selective adoption of Western military, technological, and cultural practices to strengthen the Russian state
Conversion of Russia to Catholicism
The voluntary abolition of serfdom
questions.correctText: Selective adoption of Western military, technological, and cultural practices to strengthen the Russian state

questions.prompt: The 'gunpowder empires' label applied to the Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal states emphasizes
choices: Their shared origin in Christian Europe
Their reliance on military technology, especially artillery, to expand and consolidate territorial control
Their identical religious policies
Their refusal to use gunpowder weapons
questions.correctText: Their reliance on military technology, especially artillery, to expand and consolidate territorial control

questions.prompt: The development of the caravel in fifteenth-century Iberia most directly enabled
choices: Overland conquest of Central Asia
Long-distance Atlantic exploration through improved maneuverability and ability to sail against the wind
The collapse of Mediterranean trade
Chinese expansion into the Indian Ocean
questions.correctText: Long-distance Atlantic exploration through improved maneuverability and ability to sail against the wind

questions.prompt: Vasco da Gama's 1498 arrival in Calicut is best understood as marking
choices: The first European entry into a previously isolated region
Portuguese entry into an existing, sophisticated Indian Ocean commercial system that they sought to dominate by force
The discovery of an uninhabited continent
The end of Indian Ocean trade
questions.correctText: Portuguese entry into an existing, sophisticated Indian Ocean commercial system that they sought to dominate by force

questions.prompt: Columbus's 1492 voyage and Magellan's 1519-1522 expedition shared which common motivation?
choices: Christian missionary work as the sole goal
Finding a westward sea route to the wealth of Asian markets in spices and luxury goods
Mapping the interior of Africa
Exploring the Arctic Circle
questions.correctText: Finding a westward sea route to the wealth of Asian markets in spices and luxury goods

questions.prompt: The Columbian Exchange most directly transformed global demography by
choices: Increasing indigenous American populations through European medical knowledge
Devastating indigenous American populations through Eurasian diseases while transferring New World crops such as potatoes and maize that supported Old World population growth
Eliminating the African population
Preventing migration between hemispheres
questions.correctText: Devastating indigenous American populations through Eurasian diseases while transferring New World crops such as potatoes and maize that supported Old World population growth

questions.prompt: Hernán Cortés's defeat of the Aztec Empire is best explained by which combination of factors?
choices: Spanish numerical superiority and a single decisive battle
European steel weapons, horses, the spread of smallpox, and crucial alliances with indigenous groups resentful of Aztec rule
Aztec voluntary surrender of all territory
Direct support from the Inca Empire
questions.correctText: European steel weapons, horses, the spread of smallpox, and crucial alliances with indigenous groups resentful of Aztec rule

questions.prompt: The Spanish encomienda system in the Americas is best characterized as
choices: A wage-labor system that paid indigenous workers fair market rates
A grant of indigenous labor and tribute to Spanish colonists in exchange for protection and Christianization, often resulting in coercive exploitation
A system of equal partnership between Spaniards and indigenous peoples
A purely religious institution with no economic function
questions.correctText: A grant of indigenous labor and tribute to Spanish colonists in exchange for protection and Christianization, often resulting in coercive exploitation

questions.prompt: The mita system as adapted by the Spanish in the Andes most directly served to
choices: Promote indigenous land ownership
Supply forced indigenous labor to silver mines such as Potosí, which fueled the global silver trade
Convert Andeans to Protestantism
Establish a republican government
questions.correctText: Supply forced indigenous labor to silver mines such as Potosí, which fueled the global silver trade

questions.prompt: The Atlantic slave trade's Middle Passage is best understood as
choices: A short voyage with minimal mortality
The brutal transoceanic leg of the triangular trade that forcibly transported approximately 12 million Africans to the Americas with high mortality rates
A voluntary migration of free African workers
A trade route limited to the seventeenth century
questions.correctText: The brutal transoceanic leg of the triangular trade that forcibly transported approximately 12 million Africans to the Americas with high mortality rates

questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes how Atlantic slavery differed from earlier forms of slavery in world history?
choices: It was less brutal than earlier systems
It was uniquely large-scale, racialized, hereditary, and tied to plantation production of cash crops for the Atlantic economy
It was identical to medieval European serfdom
It was abolished within a single generation
questions.correctText: It was uniquely large-scale, racialized, hereditary, and tied to plantation production of cash crops for the Atlantic economy

questions.prompt: The Manila galleon trade (1565-1815) is best described as
choices: A small regional trade with limited global impact
A long-running Spanish trade route linking Acapulco and Manila that exchanged American silver for Chinese silks and porcelain, integrating the Americas into Pacific commerce
A purely military operation with no commercial component
A British-controlled trading network
questions.correctText: A long-running Spanish trade route linking Acapulco and Manila that exchanged American silver for Chinese silks and porcelain, integrating the Americas into Pacific commerce

questions.prompt: The seventeenth-century rise of joint-stock companies such as the Dutch East India Company (VOC) is best understood as
choices: A short-lived experiment that failed within a decade
A new commercial-political form that pooled capital and could wage war, sign treaties, and govern territory in pursuit of monopoly trade
A state-owned enterprise identical to feudal estates
A purely charitable religious organization
questions.correctText: A new commercial-political form that pooled capital and could wage war, sign treaties, and govern territory in pursuit of monopoly trade

questions.prompt: Tokugawa Japan's sakoku (closed country) policy is best understood as
choices: Total isolation with no foreign contact at all
A controlled restriction on foreign contact that limited trade to designated Dutch and Chinese partners while curbing Christianity and preserving Tokugawa stability
An open-door trade policy with all Europeans
An invasion of mainland China
questions.correctText: A controlled restriction on foreign contact that limited trade to designated Dutch and Chinese partners while curbing Christianity and preserving Tokugawa stability

questions.prompt: The casta system in Spanish America most directly reflected
choices: Legal equality among colonial subjects
A racial and social hierarchy linking ancestry, occupation, and legal status that emerged from colonial mixing
Indigenous-led governance structures
Religious freedom for all groups
questions.correctText: A racial and social hierarchy linking ancestry, occupation, and legal status that emerged from colonial mixing

questions.prompt: Enlightenment thinkers such as John Locke are most associated with which political idea?
choices: The divine right of absolute monarchs
Natural rights to life, liberty, and property that governments must protect and that citizens may defend if violated
The hereditary supremacy of the Catholic Church
The rejection of all written constitutions
questions.correctText: Natural rights to life, liberty, and property that governments must protect and that citizens may defend if violated

questions.prompt: The Scientific Revolution most directly contributed to the Enlightenment by
choices: Discouraging the use of reason in public affairs
Promoting confidence that human reason and observation could uncover natural laws applicable to society and government
Reaffirming geocentric cosmology
Suppressing the printing of new ideas
questions.correctText: Promoting confidence that human reason and observation could uncover natural laws applicable to society and government

questions.prompt: The American Revolution and the French Revolution shared which common ideological foundation?
choices: A commitment to restoring medieval feudal privileges
Enlightenment principles of popular sovereignty, natural rights, and limits on monarchical power
Strict religious uniformity
The rejection of written constitutions
questions.correctText: Enlightenment principles of popular sovereignty, natural rights, and limits on monarchical power

questions.prompt: The 1789 calling of the Estates-General by Louis XVI was most directly precipitated by
choices: A French military victory in North America
A severe fiscal crisis driven by debt from wars and the privileges of the First and Second Estates
A peasant revolt in the Pacific colonies
Russian invasion of Paris
questions.correctText: A severe fiscal crisis driven by debt from wars and the privileges of the First and Second Estates

questions.prompt: The 1789 Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen is most useful as evidence of
choices: The French Revolution's commitment to restoring absolutism
The application of Enlightenment ideas about natural rights and equality before the law to a new political order
France's rejection of all written law
A continuation of feudal privilege
questions.correctText: The application of Enlightenment ideas about natural rights and equality before the law to a new political order

questions.prompt: The Haitian Revolution (1791-1804) is historiographically significant because it
choices: Was a failed peasant uprising with no lasting consequences
Produced the first independent state in Latin America led by formerly enslaved people and the only successful large-scale slave revolt to create a nation
Restored French colonial rule
Was led by European settlers seeking independence from Africa
questions.correctText: Produced the first independent state in Latin America led by formerly enslaved people and the only successful large-scale slave revolt to create a nation

questions.prompt: Simón Bolívar's leadership of Latin American independence movements is best characterized as
choices: A purely indigenous-led peasant movement
A creole-led campaign drawing on Enlightenment ideals to overthrow Spanish rule while preserving much of the existing social hierarchy
A restoration of Spanish absolutism
A pacifist movement with no military component
questions.correctText: A creole-led campaign drawing on Enlightenment ideals to overthrow Spanish rule while preserving much of the existing social hierarchy

questions.prompt: Britain's early lead in the Industrial Revolution is best explained by which combination of factors?
choices: A lack of natural resources and isolation from world trade
Access to coal and iron, a colonial-driven capital surplus, agricultural improvements, navigable rivers, and a strong patent system
The absence of any private property rights
A purely state-planned economy
questions.correctText: Access to coal and iron, a colonial-driven capital surplus, agricultural improvements, navigable rivers, and a strong patent system

questions.prompt: The factory system most directly transformed work by
choices: Returning labor to small household workshops
Concentrating wage workers in centralized sites with strict time discipline, specialized tasks, and mechanized production
Eliminating wage labor altogether
Restoring guild-based artisan production
questions.correctText: Concentrating wage workers in centralized sites with strict time discipline, specialized tasks, and mechanized production

questions.prompt: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels's 1848 'Communist Manifesto' most directly responded to which broader development?
choices: The decline of European population in the eighteenth century
The social and economic inequalities produced by industrial capitalism and the emergence of a wage-earning proletariat
The end of the trans-Atlantic slave trade
The collapse of European monarchies
questions.correctText: The social and economic inequalities produced by industrial capitalism and the emergence of a wage-earning proletariat

questions.prompt: The 'Second Industrial Revolution' (c. 1870-1914) is best distinguished from the first by its emphasis on
choices: Water power and textile manufacturing in Britain alone
Steel, chemicals, electricity, and internal-combustion engines, with industrial leadership shifting to Germany and the United States
A return to handicraft production
The dominance of agriculture over manufacturing
questions.correctText: Steel, chemicals, electricity, and internal-combustion engines, with industrial leadership shifting to Germany and the United States

questions.prompt: Nineteenth-century liberalism and conservatism are best contrasted in that liberals
choices: Sought to restore absolute monarchies and feudal privileges
Generally supported constitutional government, individual rights, and free trade, while conservatives sought to preserve traditional hierarchies and monarchical authority
Opposed all forms of representative government
Were identical to socialists
questions.correctText: Generally supported constitutional government, individual rights, and free trade, while conservatives sought to preserve traditional hierarchies and monarchical authority

questions.prompt: The unifications of Germany under Bismarck and Italy under Cavour and Garibaldi shared which common feature?
choices: They were achieved through peaceful international conferences alone
They combined nationalist sentiment with the political and military leadership of a stronger regional state to forge new nation-states by 1871
They restored medieval city-state autonomy
They were directed against the United States
questions.correctText: They combined nationalist sentiment with the political and military leadership of a stronger regional state to forge new nation-states by 1871

questions.prompt: The abolition of slavery in Britain (1833), the United States (1865), and Brazil (1888) is best understood as
choices: A coordinated international treaty in a single year
A gradual nineteenth-century process driven by humanitarian movements, slave resistance, economic shifts, and Enlightenment ideology
A reversal of earlier liberal reforms
A consequence of the Russian Revolution
questions.correctText: A gradual nineteenth-century process driven by humanitarian movements, slave resistance, economic shifts, and Enlightenment ideology

questions.prompt: Early women's suffrage and women's rights movements in the nineteenth-century West are best understood as
choices: Reactionary movements opposing the Enlightenment
Movements applying Enlightenment principles of equality and natural rights to argue for women's political and legal inclusion
State-imposed reforms unrelated to popular activism
Religious movements with no political demands
questions.correctText: Movements applying Enlightenment principles of equality and natural rights to argue for women's political and legal inclusion

questions.prompt: The 'new imperialism' of 1870-1914 differed from earlier European imperialism most importantly in its
choices: Limited geographic scope confined to the Americas
Direct political annexation of vast territories in Africa and Asia, justified by industrial needs, nationalism, and pseudoscientific racial ideologies
Total avoidance of military force
Rejection of free trade entirely
questions.correctText: Direct political annexation of vast territories in Africa and Asia, justified by industrial needs, nationalism, and pseudoscientific racial ideologies

questions.prompt: The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 is best understood as
choices: A peace treaty granting African states independence
A European diplomatic meeting that established rules for European powers to partition Africa among themselves with no African representation
An African-led pan-continental congress
A trade summit between Africa and East Asia
questions.correctText: A European diplomatic meeting that established rules for European powers to partition Africa among themselves with no African representation

questions.prompt: King Leopold II's rule of the Congo Free State is most often cited by historians as an example of
choices: A model humanitarian colonial regime
An especially brutal extractive colonialism that used forced labor and violence to obtain rubber, contributing to massive demographic catastrophe
The first decolonization of Africa
A successful free-trade zone
questions.correctText: An especially brutal extractive colonialism that used forced labor and violence to obtain rubber, contributing to massive demographic catastrophe

questions.prompt: The 1857 Sepoy Rebellion (Indian Uprising) most directly led to
choices: Indian independence from Britain
The dissolution of the British East India Company and direct rule of India by the British Crown (the Raj)
The restoration of Mughal supremacy
Russian occupation of India
questions.correctText: The dissolution of the British East India Company and direct rule of India by the British Crown (the Raj)

questions.prompt: The Opium Wars (1839-1842, 1856-1860) and the resulting 'unequal treaties' most directly demonstrated
choices: Chinese military superiority over European powers
The use of European industrial and naval power to impose extraterritoriality, treaty ports, and trade concessions on Qing China
A balanced commercial partnership between Britain and China
The end of European imperialism in East Asia
questions.correctText: The use of European industrial and naval power to impose extraterritoriality, treaty ports, and trade concessions on Qing China

questions.prompt: The Meiji Restoration of 1868 is best characterized as
choices: A peasant revolution that abolished the imperial system
A top-down, state-led modernization that selectively adopted Western industrial, military, and political models to preserve Japanese sovereignty
A return to Tokugawa isolation
A movement that ended all Japanese contact with the West
questions.correctText: A top-down, state-led modernization that selectively adopted Western industrial, military, and political models to preserve Japanese sovereignty

questions.prompt: Social Darwinism was most often used by late-nineteenth-century imperialists to
choices: Argue for the equal capacities of all peoples
Justify European racial and civilizational hierarchies and the 'right' to rule colonized populations
Oppose all forms of colonization
Promote indigenous self-determination
questions.correctText: Justify European racial and civilizational hierarchies and the 'right' to rule colonized populations

questions.prompt: The 1900 Boxer Rebellion and the Mahdist movement in Sudan are most comparable in that both
choices: Welcomed foreign rule and Christianization
Were anti-foreign, often religiously inspired resistance movements against European or European-backed encroachment
Were sponsored by the British Empire
Established lasting independent republics
questions.correctText: Were anti-foreign, often religiously inspired resistance movements against European or European-backed encroachment

questions.prompt: Nineteenth-century mass migration to the Americas, Southeast Asia, and Oceania was most directly driven by
choices: The absence of industrial economies anywhere in the world
Demand for cheap labor in industrial and plantation economies, combined with steamship and rail technology and population pressures in source regions
European bans on emigration
The end of all global trade
questions.correctText: Demand for cheap labor in industrial and plantation economies, combined with steamship and rail technology and population pressures in source regions

questions.prompt: The 'MAIN' framework for the causes of World War I emphasizes
choices: A single assassination as the only cause
Militarism, alliance systems, imperial rivalries, and nationalism as long-term underlying causes
Economic prosperity and free trade
The collapse of nationalism in Europe
questions.correctText: Militarism, alliance systems, imperial rivalries, and nationalism as long-term underlying causes

questions.prompt: World War I is often described as a 'total war' because it
choices: Was fought only by professional armies in isolated regions
Mobilized entire economies, civilian populations, and colonial subjects in support of unprecedented industrial-scale warfare
Lasted only a few weeks
Was confined to naval combat
questions.correctText: Mobilized entire economies, civilian populations, and colonial subjects in support of unprecedented industrial-scale warfare

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Versailles (1919) most directly contributed to long-term instability by
choices: Distributing reparations equally among all participants
Imposing harsh territorial losses, reparations, and a 'war guilt' clause on Germany while creating new states in Eastern Europe with contested borders
Granting Germany membership in the League of Nations immediately
Restoring all pre-war monarchies
questions.correctText: Imposing harsh territorial losses, reparations, and a 'war guilt' clause on Germany while creating new states in Eastern Europe with contested borders

questions.prompt: The 1917 Bolshevik Revolution under Lenin most directly produced
choices: A constitutional monarchy in Russia
A one-party communist state grounded in Marxist-Leninist ideology that withdrew from World War I and reorganized the Russian Empire as the USSR
A liberal democracy modeled on Britain
The restoration of the Romanov dynasty
questions.correctText: A one-party communist state grounded in Marxist-Leninist ideology that withdrew from World War I and reorganized the Russian Empire as the USSR

questions.prompt: Stalin's Five-Year Plans and forced collectivization in the 1930s are best characterized as
choices: A return to market capitalism
State-directed rapid industrialization and agricultural reorganization that achieved industrial growth at the cost of millions of lives, especially in Ukraine
Voluntary peasant cooperatives with no state coercion
A pacifist disarmament campaign
questions.correctText: State-directed rapid industrialization and agricultural reorganization that achieved industrial growth at the cost of millions of lives, especially in Ukraine

questions.prompt: Italian fascism under Mussolini and German Nazism under Hitler are most comparable in that both
choices: Embraced liberal democracy and free elections
Combined ultranationalism, single-party dictatorship, suppression of opposition, glorification of state power, and rejection of both liberalism and communism
Promoted international peace through disarmament
Rejected the use of mass propaganda
questions.correctText: Combined ultranationalism, single-party dictatorship, suppression of opposition, glorification of state power, and rejection of both liberalism and communism

questions.prompt: The Great Depression's global spread in the 1930s most directly illustrated
choices: The benefits of complete economic isolation among industrial states
The interconnected nature of the post-WWI global economy and the vulnerability of nations dependent on trade, credit, and commodity exports
The absence of any economic ties between Europe and the Americas
The triumph of laissez-faire policies
questions.correctText: The interconnected nature of the post-WWI global economy and the vulnerability of nations dependent on trade, credit, and commodity exports

questions.prompt: British and French appeasement of Nazi Germany in the late 1930s is best understood as
choices: A successful long-term peace strategy
A policy of conceding to Hitler's demands to avoid another general war, which ultimately failed to prevent World War II
An aggressive military alliance against Germany
A communist revolutionary strategy
questions.correctText: A policy of conceding to Hitler's demands to avoid another general war, which ultimately failed to prevent World War II

questions.prompt: The Holocaust is best understood by historians as
choices: An unplanned wartime accident
A systematic, state-directed genocide that murdered approximately six million Jews along with millions of Roma, disabled people, and other targeted groups
A small-scale local persecution limited to Germany
A program limited to forced emigration with no killings
questions.correctText: A systematic, state-directed genocide that murdered approximately six million Jews along with millions of Roma, disabled people, and other targeted groups

questions.prompt: The American use of atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 is most often analyzed as a turning point because it
choices: Marked the first use of gunpowder weapons in war
Ended World War II in the Pacific and inaugurated the nuclear age, reshaping postwar strategy and the emerging Cold War
Had no military or political consequences
Convinced all states to abolish weapons of mass destruction
questions.correctText: Ended World War II in the Pacific and inaugurated the nuclear age, reshaping postwar strategy and the emerging Cold War

questions.prompt: The 'bipolar' world order after 1945 is best described as
choices: A balance among many roughly equal great powers
A global ideological and strategic competition primarily between the United States and the Soviet Union and their respective blocs
A unified world government under the United Nations
A return to nineteenth-century European supremacy
questions.correctText: A global ideological and strategic competition primarily between the United States and the Soviet Union and their respective blocs

questions.prompt: The Korean War, Vietnam War, and Soviet-Afghan War are best categorized together as
choices: Conventional wars between the US and USSR directly
Cold War proxy conflicts in which the superpowers supported opposing sides in regional wars without engaging each other directly
Wars of decolonization fought only by European empires
Civil wars unconnected to global politics
questions.correctText: Cold War proxy conflicts in which the superpowers supported opposing sides in regional wars without engaging each other directly

questions.prompt: Indian independence in 1947 is best understood as
choices: A bloodless British concession with no social disruption
A negotiated decolonization shaped by mass nonviolent resistance led by figures such as Gandhi and Nehru, accompanied by the violent Hindu-Muslim partition into India and Pakistan
A British military victory that retained colonial rule
A communist revolution led by Mao
questions.correctText: A negotiated decolonization shaped by mass nonviolent resistance led by figures such as Gandhi and Nehru, accompanied by the violent Hindu-Muslim partition into India and Pakistan

questions.prompt: Ghana's 1957 independence under Kwame Nkrumah is historiographically significant because it
choices: Was the first European decolonization in the Americas
Was the first sub-Saharan African colony to gain independence from European rule, inspiring further African decolonization movements
Restored European colonial rule across Africa
Was achieved through Soviet military invasion
questions.correctText: Was the first sub-Saharan African colony to gain independence from European rule, inspiring further African decolonization movements

questions.prompt: The Algerian War of Independence (1954-1962) and the Vietnam War (Vietnam vs. France, 1946-1954) are best compared as
choices: Peaceful negotiated transitions to independence
Prolonged armed struggles against French colonial rule that helped accelerate the broader collapse of European empires
Conflicts that restored French Indochina and French Algeria
Wars led by European settler movements against indigenous resistance
questions.correctText: Prolonged armed struggles against French colonial rule that helped accelerate the broader collapse of European empires

questions.prompt: South African apartheid (1948-1994) is best characterized as
choices: A purely economic policy with no racial component
A legal system of racial segregation and white-minority rule that was ultimately dismantled through internal resistance, international sanctions, and negotiated transition
A federal arrangement granting all groups equal rights
A Soviet-imposed system
questions.correctText: A legal system of racial segregation and white-minority rule that was ultimately dismantled through internal resistance, international sanctions, and negotiated transition

questions.prompt: Nelson Mandela's role in ending apartheid is most often interpreted as that of
choices: A military strongman who imposed minority rule
A figure who combined resistance leadership with later reconciliation, helping negotiate a peaceful transition to multiracial democratic South Africa
A colonial administrator
An apolitical religious leader
questions.correctText: A figure who combined resistance leadership with later reconciliation, helping negotiate a peaceful transition to multiracial democratic South Africa

questions.prompt: Mao Zedong's Cultural Revolution (1966-1976) is best understood as
choices: A moderate liberalization of Chinese society
A radical Maoist political campaign that mobilized youth Red Guards, purged perceived enemies, attacked traditional culture and intellectuals, and caused widespread upheaval and violence
A peaceful agrarian reform with no political content
A capitalist economic reform program
questions.correctText: A radical Maoist political campaign that mobilized youth Red Guards, purged perceived enemies, attacked traditional culture and intellectuals, and caused widespread upheaval and violence

questions.prompt: The 1979 Iranian Revolution is best characterized as
choices: A secular liberal democratic revolution
A revolution that overthrew the US-backed Shah and replaced his regime with an Islamic Republic led by Ayatollah Khomeini, marking a turning point in political Islam and Middle Eastern geopolitics
A communist takeover allied with Moscow
A restoration of the Qajar dynasty
questions.correctText: A revolution that overthrew the US-backed Shah and replaced his regime with an Islamic Republic led by Ayatollah Khomeini, marking a turning point in political Islam and Middle Eastern geopolitics

questions.prompt: Mikhail Gorbachev's policies of glasnost and perestroika in the late 1980s most directly contributed to
choices: A consolidation of Stalinist central planning
Domestic openness and economic restructuring whose unintended consequences accelerated the collapse of communist regimes in Eastern Europe and the 1991 dissolution of the USSR
The expansion of the Soviet bloc into Western Europe
A military victory in Afghanistan
questions.correctText: Domestic openness and economic restructuring whose unintended consequences accelerated the collapse of communist regimes in Eastern Europe and the 1991 dissolution of the USSR

questions.prompt: The Cambodian genocide under the Khmer Rouge (1975-1979) and the Rwandan genocide of 1994 are most comparable in that both
choices: Were entirely the work of foreign invasion forces
Involved organized mass killings driven by ideological or ethnic targeting and resulted in the deaths of approximately one to two million or more people each
Were quickly stopped by United Nations peacekeepers
Targeted only military personnel
questions.correctText: Involved organized mass killings driven by ideological or ethnic targeting and resulted in the deaths of approximately one to two million or more people each

questions.prompt: Late-twentieth-century globalization is best characterized by
choices: A retreat from international trade and migration
Accelerated flows of capital, goods, people, and information enabled by container shipping, jet travel, telecommunications, and trade liberalization
The elimination of multinational corporations
A return to mercantilist autarky
questions.correctText: Accelerated flows of capital, goods, people, and information enabled by container shipping, jet travel, telecommunications, and trade liberalization

questions.prompt: The World Trade Organization, NAFTA, and the European Union are best understood collectively as
choices: Cold War military alliances
Institutional and regional frameworks promoting trade liberalization and economic integration in the late twentieth century
Religious organizations governing pilgrimage
Anti-globalization movements
questions.correctText: Institutional and regional frameworks promoting trade liberalization and economic integration in the late twentieth century

questions.prompt: The rise of multinational corporations in the late twentieth century most directly contributed to
choices: A decrease in international flows of capital
The reorganization of production into globally distributed supply chains and the increasing economic power of firms operating across many states
The end of all international trade
A return to pre-industrial cottage production
questions.correctText: The reorganization of production into globally distributed supply chains and the increasing economic power of firms operating across many states

questions.prompt: The late-twentieth-century internet revolution most directly transformed global culture and economics by
choices: Slowing the spread of information
Enabling instantaneous global communication, e-commerce, and the rapid diffusion of cultural products, while raising new questions about surveillance and inequality
Eliminating all linguistic differences worldwide
Restoring print as the dominant medium
questions.correctText: Enabling instantaneous global communication, e-commerce, and the rapid diffusion of cultural products, while raising new questions about surveillance and inequality

questions.prompt: International agreements such as the Kyoto Protocol and the Paris Agreement most directly respond to
choices: Cold War nuclear arms races
The challenge of anthropogenic climate change driven by industrial greenhouse gas emissions
The collapse of the Bretton Woods system
Religious sectarian conflict
questions.correctText: The challenge of anthropogenic climate change driven by industrial greenhouse gas emissions

questions.prompt: The September 11, 2001 attacks and the subsequent US-led 'War on Terror' most directly reshaped twenty-first-century geopolitics by
choices: Ending all US military involvement abroad
Triggering prolonged US-led military interventions in Afghanistan and Iraq, expanding global counterterrorism efforts, and reframing security and civil-liberties debates
Producing a peaceful disarmament of the Middle East
Restoring the Cold War bipolar order intact
questions.correctText: Triggering prolonged US-led military interventions in Afghanistan and Iraq, expanding global counterterrorism efforts, and reframing security and civil-liberties debates

questions.prompt: The Arab Spring uprisings of 2010-2012 are best characterized as
choices: A purely religious movement seeking to restore caliphates

A wave of mass protests across the Middle East and North Africa demanding political reform and economic opportunity, producing varied outcomes from democratic transition to civil war and authoritarian retrenchment
A coordinated military invasion by Western powers
A peaceful unified transition to liberal democracy across the region
questions.correctText: A wave of mass protests across the Middle East and North Africa demanding political reform and economic opportunity, producing varied outcomes from democratic transition to civil war and authoritarian retrenchment

questions.prompt: The COVID-19 pandemic that began in 2019-2020 is most useful to historians of the long twenty-first century as evidence of
choices: The unimportance of global interconnection
How densely interconnected global travel, trade, and supply chains can transmit a pathogen worldwide and stress political, economic, and public-health systems simultaneously
The end of all globalization
The disappearance of nation-state governance
questions.correctText: How densely interconnected global travel, trade, and supply chains can transmit a pathogen worldwide and stress political, economic, and public-health systems simultaneously

questions.prompt: From a long-term historiographical perspective, 1750-1900 is best characterized by
choices: Stagnation in technology and political ideology
Intersecting revolutions—political, industrial, and ideological—that reorganized labor, state power, and global hierarchies and produced the modern imperial world order
A return to medieval feudalism worldwide
The complete absence of state-led modernization outside Europe
questions.correctText: Intersecting revolutions—political, industrial, and ideological—that reorganized labor, state power, and global hierarchies and produced the modern imperial world order

questions.prompt: Which of the following best captures a major historiographical debate about European overseas expansion after 1450?
choices: Whether Europeans ever traveled outside Europe

Whether European dominance arose primarily from internal European factors, conjunctural advantages such as American silver and Eurasian disease ecology, or coercive integration into pre-existing global networks
Whether African societies existed at all before 1450
Whether the Columbian Exchange ever occurred
questions.correctText: Whether European dominance arose primarily from internal European factors, conjunctural advantages such as American silver and Eurasian disease ecology, or coercive integration into pre-existing global networks

questions.prompt: Comparing pre-1450 Eurasian trade networks (Silk Roads, Indian Ocean, trans-Saharan) with the post-1500 Atlantic system, the Atlantic system was most distinctive in its
choices: Reliance on monsoon winds and camels
Coupling of European-controlled maritime power with large-scale, racialized, coerced African labor on New World plantations producing commodities for European markets
Limited geographic scope confined to one ocean basin
Absence of long-distance commercial exchange
questions.correctText: Coupling of European-controlled maritime power with large-scale, racialized, coerced African labor on New World plantations producing commodities for European markets
